root_text.sn56.47:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso mahāsamudde ekacchiggaḷaṁ yugaṁ pakkhipeyya. Tatrāpissa kāṇo kacchapo. So vassasatassa vassasatassa accayena sakiṁ sakiṁ ummujjeyya.
root_text.sn56.47:1.3: api nu kho kāṇo kacchapo vassasatassa vassasatassa accayena sakiṁ sakiṁ ummujjanto amusmiṁ ekacchiggaḷe yuge gīvaṁ paveseyyā”ti?
root_text.sn56.47:1.4: “Yadi nūna, bhante, kadāci karahaci dīghassa addhuno accayenā”ti.
root_text.sn56.47:2.1: “Khippataraṁ kho so, bhikkhave, kāṇo kacchapo vassasatassa vassasatassa accayena sakiṁ sakiṁ ummujjanto amusmiṁ ekacchiggaḷe yuge gīvaṁ paveseyya, na tvevāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sakiṁ vinipātagatena bālena manussattaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.sn56.47:3.2: Na hettha, bhikkhave, atthi dhammacariyā, samacariyā, kusalakiriyā, puññakiriyā.
root_text.sn56.47:4.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.47:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose a person were to throw a yoke with a single hole into the ocean. And there was a one-eyed turtle who popped up once every hundred years.
translation_text.sn56.47:1.3: Would that one-eyed turtle, popping up once every hundred years, still poke its neck through the hole in that yoke?”
translation_text.sn56.47:1.4: “Only after a very long time, sir, if ever.”
translation_text.sn56.47:2.1: “That one-eyed turtle might poke its neck through the hole in that yoke, but in any case it’d be sooner than a fool fallen to the underworld is reborn as a human being, I say.
translation_text.sn56.47:3.2: Because in that place there’s no principled or moral conduct, and no doing what is good and skillful.
translation_text.sn56.47:3.3: There they just prey on each other, preying on the weak.
translation_text.sn56.47:3.5: It’s because they haven’t seen the four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.47:3.7: The noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.47:4.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”

variant_text.sn56.47:2.1: vinipātagatena bālena → vinipātaṁ gatena bālena (bj); vinītagatena bahulena (mr)
